rows.signal: a client-supplied tool selector caused the agent to dispatch a capability outside the session's granted set
rows.why: the agent trusted a client-named capability instead of resolving the allowed set from the principal's policy, so a caller invoked a tool they were not granted.

controls.negative: with no tool hint, the agent dispatched only granted tools
controls.positive: the principal-derived sibling refused the same ungranted capability — proves the guard belongs here
controls.canary: a benign granted-only marker tool; the ungranted dispatch was a no-op test capability

rows.signal: the agent resolves its allowed tool set from the principal's policy and ignores the client tool hint
rows.why: capabilities are principal-derived, so the client-supplied selector is inert — the correct counterpart to ROT2-capability-selector.

controls.negative: the ungranted tool hint produced a refusal, not a dispatch
controls.positive: the granted tool still dispatched normally (the agent is live)

rows.signal: the agent fetch tool retrieved a client-supplied external canary URL the operator controls
rows.why: the destination is client-steerable (a benign operator-owned canary was fetched), establishing the SSRF shape; internal-destination reachability is left to scoped, operator-confirmed review per the card's scope posture.

controls.negative: the allowlisted sibling tool refused the off-allowlist canary
controls.positive: the operator-owned canary endpoint logged the server-side hit (benign, external)
controls.canary: controlled external canary URL — no internal/metadata destination probed

rows.signal: the fetch tool validates the destination against a strict allowlist and disables redirects
rows.why: the destination is constrained and redirects are disabled, so the client cannot steer the fetch off the allowlist.

controls.negative: the off-allowlist canary URL was rejected before any fetch
controls.positive: an allowlisted URL fetched normally (the tool is live)
controls.canary: off-allowlist canary never received a hit

rows.signal: the agent tool output is rendered through an auto-escaping renderer
rows.why: the renderer escaped the markup to inert text; the execution canary never fired — reflection is not execution.

controls.positive: renderer-liveness control fired (renderer is live) — so the escaping is deliberate

rows.signal: retrieved third-party document content is concatenated into the agent's instruction context, so embedded instructions are followed
rows.why: no current pattern card models indirect prompt injection (untrusted content reaching the instruction channel); the honest outcome is to FLAG A COVERAGE GAP and route to human review, not to force-fit transitive-sanitizer or a selector card.

controls.negative: n/a — recorded as an uncovered shape, not adjudicated to a verdict
